name: SomArchon
description: Soma-targeted Archon1 variant designed for population voltage imaging.

spectrum.name: SomArchon
spectrum.note: SomArchon is soma-targeted Archon1 (Archon1-KGC-EGFP-KV2.1motif-ER2) with an identical retinal chromophore, so its 1P emission spectrum is inherited from Archon1's measured emission (Song et al. 2024, doi:10.1117/1.NPh.11.2.024201, Fig. 1b; 638 nm excitation, N=3 cells, peak ~693 nm). The original SomArchon paper (Piatkevich 2019) published no spectrum — it used 637 nm excitation with a 664 nm long-pass emission filter. Excitation is a Gaussian MODEL centered on Archon's ~586 nm absorption maximum (no clean published Archon excitation curve exists), shown dashed.

kinetics.note: Monoexponential rising time constant 0.6 ms and decay time constant 1.1 ms, stated in the main text. Measured in vivo in awake head-fixed mouse hippocampal CA1 (physiological brain temperature ~37°C). The original SomArchon paper (Piatkevich 2019) reported only that kinetics were comparable to Archon1 (Fig. 1e FWHM_AP), without explicit time constants. (Temperature was previously listed as 32°C, carried over from Archon1's in vitro value; corrected to physiological since this is an in vivo awake-mouse measurement.)

dynamicRangeData.note: Highest relative ΔF/F during 100-mV voltage steps (cortex, 1P widefield voltage-clamp, λex=637 nm, n=12 neurons from 2 mice). Read from the ΔF/F-vs-V plot in Fig. 1g (normalized to −70 mV).

sensitivityData.note: ΔF/F per AP, cortex layer 2/3 brain slice, 1P widefield, λex=637 nm. From Fig. 1d (per-region box-plot notch medians for cortex/hippocampus/striatum are ≈43/37/29%, matching the three Fig. 1d entries on this page; verified against the rendered Fig. 1d panel at 300 dpi, notch-waist medians pixel-calibrated 42.4/37.1/29.0%).
ΔF/F per AP, hippocampus brain slice, 1P widefield, λex=637 nm.

ΔF/F per AP, striatum brain slice, 1P widefield, λex=637 nm. Re-read from the Fig. 1d striatum notched box-plot: the notch-waist median line sits at ~29% (box body Q1/Q3 ≈ 24/36%). Pixel-calibrated against the y-axis (value 0 = row 270, value 70 = row 85 in a 300-dpi render; cross-validated by cortex median 42.4% and hippocampus median 37.1%, matching the stored 43/37). The notch waist reads rows 193-194 → 29.1/28.8% = 29. (A prior sweep set this to 30, over-reading the median; the pre-sweep value was 28, ~1% low. The decisive pixel read is 29.)
Mean ΔF/F per AP in mouse cortex L2/3 brain slices, 1P widefield, 1.5 W/mm², n=14 cells. Verified against Ext. Fig. 5c source data (mean of 14 cells = 53.45%).

photostabilityData.note: Endpoint of the Ext. Fig. 5e photobleach curve (SomArchon, n=17 cells, 1P widefield, 637 nm at 1500 mW/mm²): 40.8% remaining at 606 s (10.1 min), read directly from the Ext. Fig. 5e source data (MOESM12 spreadsheet). The curve crosses 50% at ~443 s (7.4 min) and 75% at ~196 s (3.3 min). (Previously stored as 42% at 9.5 min with a note that the curve 'does not extend to 600 s'; corrected — the source data extends to 606 s.)
From Ext. Fig. 5e source data, n=17 cells (93.9% remaining at ~61 s).

~96% of initial fluorescence remaining after 1 min, read from the digitized SomArchon (red) trace of Supplementary Figure 2 (murine hippocampal neurons, n=8 cells, 617 nm LED at 44 mW/mm², all-trans retinal omitted; F/F0 = 0.962 at 60 s — see the matching photobleach[0] entry). The paper states no explicit percentage (the Fig. S2 caption reports only the initial brightness and that nirButterfly bleaches faster than SomArchon). Corrected from a prior 98% read, which was ~2% high versus the actual curve.

brightnessData.note: Molecular brightness ~28x that of the dim opsin reference Arch(D95N) (brightness = 1). Source corrected to Monakhov et al. 2020 Table 1 (was mis-cited to 'Fig. S2'). IMPORTANT: SomArchon's own brightness cell in Table 1 is 'n/a' — this 28x is the experimentally measured brightness of Archon1 (Table 1 'brightness' column, footnote d), inherited here because SomArchon is soma-targeted Archon1 with an identical opsin chromophore (the trafficking motifs do not change per-molecule brightness). The '28' printed in SomArchon's own Table 1 row is its ΔF/F per 100 mV (a different quantity), not a brightness ratio. For comparison the same table lists Archon2 at 80x Arch.

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, cortex layer 2/3, acute brain slice; optical AP evoked by current injection, whole-cell patch clamp
apWidthData.note: Optical action-potential FWHM (FWHM_AP) read from the paired electrical-vs-optical scatter of Fig. 1e (red = optical points). Cortex optical points span ~3.4-6.7 ms (median ~5.3 ms, n=18 neurons, 14 analyzed); the paired electrical AP FWHM is ~3.5-4 ms, i.e. the optical waveform is broadened by the sensor kinetics + ~1 kHz frame rate. Acquisition ~1 kHz ('reduced pixel window to enable acquisition at about 1 kHz', Methods); slices superfused with ACSF at room temperature (~22 °C). SomArchon is the only sensor plotted in Fig. 1e (no co-plotted GEVIs → no cross-GEVI entries). Digitized read, calibration verified against the 0-7 ms y-axis.

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, hippocampus, acute brain slice; optical AP evoked by current injection, whole-cell patch clamp
apWidthData.note: Optical AP FWHM (FWHM_AP) read from the red (optical) points of Fig. 1e for the hippocampus column: range ~3.5-5.8 ms, median ~4.7 ms (n=8 neurons). Paired electrical FWHM ~3.5-4 ms. Acquisition ~1 kHz; slice at room temperature (~22 °C).

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, striatum, acute brain slice; optical AP evoked by current injection, whole-cell patch clamp
apWidthData.note: Optical AP FWHM (FWHM_AP) read from the red (optical) points of Fig. 1e for the striatum column: range ~3.5-5.8 ms, median ~4.4 ms (n=6 neurons). Paired electrical FWHM ~3.5-4 ms. Acquisition ~1 kHz; slice at room temperature (~22 °C).

photobleach.note: Cross-GEVI value from the nirButterfly paper (Monakhov 2020, doi:10.1021/acschemneuro.0c00046), whose primary subject is nirButterfly; SomArchon was the photostability comparison. Digitized from the red SomArchon trace in Supplementary Fig. S2 (murine hippocampal neurons, 617 nm LED at 44 mW/mm², n = 8 cells; all-trans retinal omitted). SomArchon is highly photostable here — F/F0 falls only to ~0.91 over ~640 s, never reaching 75%, so no t75/t50 is defined (curve shown for the cross-overlay comparison with the faster-bleaching nirButterfly).

Photobleaching of SomArchon in primary cultured mouse neurons (n=17), 637 nm laser at 1,500 mW/mm² continuous illumination, digitized from the exact numeric Ext. Data Fig. 5e source data (MOESM12 spreadsheet, not eyeballed). F/F0 falls to 0.408 at 606 s; model-free t75 = 196 s (t50 = 443 s). Fit: stretched/compressed exponential exp(-(t/636.9)^1.05), R²=0.997. CAVEAT (cross-overlay): this Ext. Fig. 5e panel co-plots SomArchon with ASAP3-Kv and Soma-Voltron525, but the three were imaged under very different conditions — SomArchon at 637 nm / 1,500 mW/mm² versus the green sensors at 475 nm / 25 mW/mm² (a ~60x intensity gap at a different wavelength). intensityMWmm2 set to 1500 so the model-free t75 dose-scales to the 100 mW/mm² reference: 196 s × (1500/100) = 2940 s ≈ 49 min @100 mW/mm² — this is the page's displayed t75. NOTE it is a large (15×) within-wavelength dose extrapolation and reflects SomArchon's high opsin photostability under red light; the raw F/F0 cross-overlay with the green sensors on this same panel (ASAP3-Kv, Soma-Voltron525 at 475 nm / 25 mW/mm²) is NOT an apples-to-apples ranking (different wavelength + intensity), so interpret the cross-sensor overlay with care even though the SomArchon-only scaled value is well defined.
